author: e-ddykim
title: JS API E2E test fails - Electron binary download fetch failed on Windows
signature: electron failed to install correctly fetch failed|Network

failed_jobs.name: JS API (24)
failed_jobs.symptom: E2E test 'should run electron package and verify output' failed: Electron binary download returned TypeError: fetch failed

key_errors:
exec error: Error: Command failed: cd demo-electron-app-project && npm start
TypeError: fetch failed
Error: Electron failed to install correctly. Please delete `node_modules/electron` and run "npx install-electron --no" manually.

1 passing (10s)
1 failing
Error: Command failed: cd demo-electron-app-project && npm start
TypeError: fetch failed
root_cause: The Electron binary download failed with 'TypeError: fetch failed' during the npm install step of the demo-electron-app-project in the JS binding E2E test on Windows. The Electron package's post-install script fetches the Electron binary from GitHub releases; the network fetch failed (likely a transient network error or connectivity issue on the Windows CI runner). This is unrelated to the PR changes (GPU ConvertMatMulToFullyConnected fix).
reproduction_steps: N/A - transient network failure on CI Windows runner
recommended_actions: Re-run the failed jobs - this is a transient network failure
If recurs frequently, consider pre-caching the Electron binary on Windows runners
prevention_strategies: Cache the Electron binary in the CI environment to avoid network dependency during tests. Consider using a local mirror or artifact cache for the Electron binary download.
historical_context: First recorded occurrence of this specific Electron fetch-failed pattern in the CI Doctor MQ database.
